title: Human Instrumentality Project
wikitext:
[]: Human Instrumentality Project             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:09 am After seeing End of Eva, wow awesome. But in comparison to the TV show, how is the Instrumentality Project initiated? Was this done prior to EoE starting and thats why we see Shinji so helpless at the beginning? I know Asuka is in that coma possibly because of being mind fucked by Arael. Can anyone clear some of that up?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:14 am As far as we can tell it happened right after 24. As far the reasons why it started, it remains vague, but I believe it points to Gendo being heavily involved in the project this time around. Unless you want to go with that theory that EoTV coincided with EoE, then it happened while Instrumentality occurred in EoE. Which, as far as I know, is the day after 24.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:20 am Ok good, but as far as the Instrumentality process itself, is it a simulator room they were placed in?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:23 am ProfessorKaos64 wrote: Ok good, but as far as the Instrumentality process itself, is it a simulator room they were placed in?

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:24 am I apologize for being a n00b, but what or who is tang?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:25 am ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I apologize for being a n00b, but what or who is tang?

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:32 am Haha yea I know , I was referring to what really initiated that "self appraisal" process that Shinji and others went through. In EoTV, his whole battle with his mind, what physical process starts that or do we never know? Did they just throw him in a cage and have him battle it out, or did he just break down one day?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:39 pm Sachi wrote: the day after 24.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Thu Apr 22, 2010 5:39 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I apologize for being a n00b, but what or who is tang?

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 8:15 pm Mr. Tines wrote: @ProfessorKaos64 Tang

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 8:21 pm Speaking of Instrumentality, it appears that another Evangelion Asuka/Rei thread on /a/ was tanged.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Apr 23, 2010 12:52 am Tines does point out that the first day of EoE is extremely rushed if it's ALL after 24, but that's how it goes. One of the first scenes involves the crew wondering what happens now that all the Angels have been defeated, and surprise that they're still on Nerv's equivalent of Red Alert.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Apr 23, 2010 8:06 am Legendary wrote: Tines does point out that the first day of EoE is extremely rushed if it's ALL after 24, but that's how it goes. One of the first scenes involves the crew wondering what happens now that all the Angels have been defeated, and surprise that they're still on Nerv's equivalent of Red Alert.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 10:12 am Legendary wrote: Tines does point out that the first day of EoE is extremely rushed if it's ALL after 24, but that's how it goes. One of the first scenes involves the crew wondering what happens now that all the Angels have been defeated, and surprise that they're still on Nerv's equivalent of Red Alert.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Sun Apr 25, 2010 6:24 am The way, I see it, episodes 25 and 26 are what's going on in Shinji's mind during Instrumentality, and the 'Congratulations!' bit at the end is basically his decision to return. EoE runs on directly from the end of episode 24. I think.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 25, 2010 1:33 pm Absolute minimum of one day passes, due to it being day when Shinji attempts a one-way swim, night when his batteries run out, and day again when the attack occurs... but it's certainly possible that a few days have gone by in a blur.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Sun Apr 25, 2010 2:52 pm For a second, I thought this thread was gonna be about... that
